fields.name: top_logprobs
fields.description:
An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the maximum number of most likely
tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log
probability. In some cases, the number of returned tokens may be fewer than
requested.
`logprobs` must be set to `true` if this parameter is used.

fields.name: user
fields.description:
This field is being replaced by `safety_identifier` and `prompt_cache_key`. Use `prompt_cache_key` instead to maintain caching optimizations.
A stable identifier for your end-users.
Used to boost cache hit rates by better bucketing similar requests and  to help OpenAI detect and prevent abuse. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

fields.name: safety_identifier
fields.description:
A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies.
The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user, with a maximum length of 64 characters. We recommend hashing their username or email address, in order to avoid sending us any identifying information. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

fields.name: prompt_cache_key
fields.description: Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates. Replaces the `user` field. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching).

fields.name: messages
fields.description:
A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Depending on the
[model](/docs/models) you use, different message types (modalities) are
supported, like [text](/docs/guides/text-generation),
[images](/docs/guides/vision), and [audio](/docs/guides/audio).

fields.name: max_completion_tokens
fields.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and [reasoning tokens](/docs/guides/reasoning).

fields.name: frequency_penalty
fields.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on
their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's
likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

fields.name: presence_penalty
fields.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on
whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood
to talk about new topics.

fields.name: web_search_options
fields.description:
This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a response.
Learn more about the [web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search?api-mode=chat).

fields.name: response_format
fields.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

fields.name: audio
fields.description:
Parameters for audio output. Required when audio output is requested with
`modalities: ["audio"]`. [Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

fields.name: store
fields.description:
Whether or not to store the output of this chat completion request for
use in our [model distillation](/docs/guides/distillation) or
[evals](/docs/guides/evals) products.

Supports text and image inputs. Note: image inputs over 8MB will be dropped.

fields.name: stream
fields.description:
If set to true, the model response data will be streamed to the client
as it is generated using [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#Event_stream_format).
See the [Streaming section below](/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming)
for more information, along with the [streaming responses](/docs/guides/streaming-responses)
guide for more information on how to handle the streaming events.

fields.name: logit_bias
fields.description:
Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion.

Accepts a JSON object that maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the
tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically,
the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling.
The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should
decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100
should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

fields.name: logprobs
fields.description:
Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true,
returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the
`content` of `message`.

fields.name: max_tokens
fields.description:
The maximum number of [tokens](/tokenizer) that can be generated in the
chat completion. This value can be used to control
[costs](https://openai.com/api/pricing/) for text generated via API.

This value is now deprecated in favor of `max_completion_tokens`, and is
not compatible with [o-series models](/docs/guides/reasoning).

fields.name: n
fields.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep `n` as `1` to minimize costs.

fields.name: prediction
fields.description:
Configuration for a [Predicted Output](/docs/guides/predicted-outputs),
which can greatly improve response times when large parts of the model
response are known ahead of time. This is most common when you are
regenerating a file with only minor changes to most of the content.

fields.name: seed
fields.description:
This feature is in Beta.
If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same `seed` and parameters should return the same result.
Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the `system_fingerprint` response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
A list of tools the model may call. You can provide either
[custom tools](/docs/guides/function-calling#custom-tools) or
[function tools](/docs/guides/function-calling).

fields.name: function_call
fields.description:
Deprecated in favor of `tool_choice`.

Controls which (if any) function is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a
message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a
function.

Specifying a particular function via `{"name": "my_function"}` forces the
model to call that function.

`none` is the default when no functions are present. `auto` is the default
if functions are present.

fields.name: functions
fields.description:
Deprecated in favor of `tools`.

A list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

types.name: ServiceTier
types.description:
Specifies the processing type used for serving the request.
  - If set to 'auto', then the request will be processed with the service tier configured in the Project settings. Unless otherwise configured, the Project will use 'default'.
  - If set to 'default', then the request will be processed with the standard pricing and performance for the selected model.
  - If set to '[flex](/docs/guides/flex-processing)' or '[priority](https://openai.com/api-priority-processing/)', then the request will be processed with the corresponding service tier.
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When the `service_tier` parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` value based on the processing mode actually used to serve the request. This response value may be different from the value set in the parameter.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestPromptCacheRetention
types.description: The retention policy for the prompt cache. Set to `24h` to enable extended prompt caching, which keeps cached prefixes active for longer, up to a maximum of 24 hours. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching#prompt-cache-retention).

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestDeveloperMessage

fields.name: content
fields.description: The contents of the developer message.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `developer`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

types.description:
Developer-provided instructions that the model should follow, regardless of
messages sent by the user. With o1 models and newer, `developer` messages
replace the previous `system` messages.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestDeveloperMessageContent
types.description: The contents of the developer message.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the content part.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text content.

types.description: Learn about [text inputs](/docs/guides/text-generation).

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestSystemMessage

fields.name: content
fields.description: The contents of the system message.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `system`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

types.description:
Developer-provided instructions that the model should follow, regardless of
messages sent by the user. With o1 models and newer, use `developer` messages
for this purpose instead.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestSystemMessageContent
types.description: The contents of the system message.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestUserMessage

fields.name: content
fields.description: The contents of the user message.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `user`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

types.description:
Messages sent by an end user, containing prompts or additional context
information.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestUserMessageContent
types.description: The contents of the user message.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartImage

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the content part.

types.description: Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

fields.name: url
fields.description: Either a URL of the image or the base64 encoded image data.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Specifies the detail level of the image. Learn more in the [Vision guide](/docs/guides/vision#low-or-high-fidelity-image-understanding).

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartImageImageUrlDetail
types.description: Specifies the detail level of the image. Learn more in the [Vision guide](/docs/guides/vision#low-or-high-fidelity-image-understanding).

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartAudio

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the content part. Always `input_audio`.

types.description: Learn about [audio inputs](/docs/guides/audio).

fields.name: data
fields.description: Base64 encoded audio data.

fields.name: format
fields.description: The format of the encoded audio data. Currently supports "wav" and "mp3".

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartAudioInputAudioFormat
types.description: The format of the encoded audio data. Currently supports "wav" and "mp3".

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartFile

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the content part. Always `file`.

types.description: Learn about [file inputs](/docs/guides/text) for text generation.

fields.name: filename
fields.description:
The name of the file, used when passing the file to the model as a 
string.

fields.name: file_data
fields.description:
The base64 encoded file data, used when passing the file to the model 
as a string.

fields.name: file_id
fields.description: The ID of an uploaded file to use as input.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestAssistantMessage

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `assistant`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

types.description: Messages sent by the model in response to user messages.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestAssistantMessageContent
types.description: The contents of the assistant message. Required unless `tool_calls` or `function_call` is specified.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the content part.

fields.name: refusal
fields.description: The refusal message generated by the model.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestAssistantMessageAudio

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for a previous audio response from the model.

types.description:
Data about a previous audio response from the model.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

types.name: ChatCompletionMessageToolCall

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the tool call.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

fields.name: function
fields.description: The function that the model called.

types.description: A call to a function tool created by the model.

types.name: ChatCompletionMessageToolCallFunction

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

types.description: The function that the model called.

types.name: ChatCompletionMessageCustomToolCall

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the tool call.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Always `custom`.

fields.name: custom
fields.description: The custom tool that the model called.

types.description: A call to a custom tool created by the model.

types.name: ChatCompletionMessageCustomToolCallCustom

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The input for the custom tool call generated by the model.

types.description: The custom tool that the model called.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestAssistantMessageFunctionCall

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.description: Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`. The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `tool`.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The contents of the tool message.

fields.name: tool_call_id
fields.description: Tool call that this message is responding to.

types.name: ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContent
types.description: The contents of the tool message.

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.name: Verbosity
types.description:
Constrains the verbosity of the model's response. Lower values will result in
more concise responses, while higher values will result in more verbose responses.
Currently supported values are `low`, `medium`, and `high`.

types.name: ReasoningEffort
types.description:
Constrains effort on reasoning for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

- `gpt-5.1` defaults to `none`, which does not perform reasoning. The supported reasoning values for `gpt-5.1` are `none`, `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Tool calls are supported for all reasoning values in gpt-5.1.
- All models before `gpt-5.1` default to `medium` reasoning effort, and do not support `none`.
- The `gpt-5-pro` model defaults to (and only supports) `high` reasoning effort.
- `xhigh` is supported for all models after `gpt-5.1-codex-max`.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestWebSearchOptions

fields.name: user_location
fields.description: Approximate location parameters for the search.

types.description:
This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a response.
Learn more about the [web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search?api-mode=chat).

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestWebSearchOptionsUserLocation

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

types.description: Approximate location parameters for the search.

types.name: WebSearchLocation

fields.name: country
fields.description:
The two-letter 
[ISO country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) of the user,
e.g. `US`.

fields.name: region
fields.description: Free text input for the region of the user, e.g. `California`.

fields.name: city
fields.description: Free text input for the city of the user, e.g. `San Francisco`.

fields.name: timezone
fields.description:
The [IANA timezone](https://timeapi.io/documentation/iana-timezones) 
of the user, e.g. `America/Los_Angeles`.

types.description: Approximate location parameters for the search.

types.name: WebSearchContextSize
types.description:
High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the 
search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestResponseFormat
types.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

types.name: ResponseFormatText

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

types.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchema

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

fields.name: json_schema
fields.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

types.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonSchemaJsonSchema

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

types.name: ResponseFormatJsonObject

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

types.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestAudio

fields.name: format
fields.description:
Specifies the output audio format. Must be one of `wav`, `mp3`, `flac`,
`opus`, or `pcm16`.

types.description:
Parameters for audio output. Required when audio output is requested with
`modalities: ["audio"]`. [Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoice
types.description: A built-in voice name or a custom voice reference.

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoiceVariant1

fields.name: id
fields.description: The custom voice ID, e.g. `voice_1234`.

types.description: Custom voice reference.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestAudioFormat
types.description:
Specifies the output audio format. Must be one of `wav`, `mp3`, `flac`,
`opus`, or `pcm16`.

types.name: StopConfiguration
types.description:
Not supported with latest reasoning models `o3` and `o4-mini`.

Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The
returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

types.name: PredictionContent

fields.name: type
fields.description:
The type of the predicted content you want to provide. This type is
currently always `content`.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
The content that should be matched when generating a model response.
If generated tokens would match this content, the entire model response
can be returned much more quickly.

types.description:
Static predicted output content, such as the content of a text file that is
being regenerated.

types.name: ChatCompletionStreamOptions

fields.name: include_usage
fields.description:
If set, an additional chunk will be streamed before the `data: [DONE]`
message. The `usage` field on this chunk shows the token usage statistics
for the entire request, and the `choices` field will always be an empty
array.

All other chunks will also include a `usage` field, but with a null
value. **NOTE:** If the stream is interrupted, you may not receive the
final usage chunk which contains the total token usage for the request.

fields.name: include_obfuscation
fields.description:
When true, stream obfuscation will be enabled. Stream obfuscation adds
random characters to an `obfuscation` field on streaming delta events to
normalize payload sizes as a mitigation to certain side-channel attacks.
These obfuscation fields are included by default, but add a small amount
of overhead to the data stream. You can set `include_obfuscation` to
false to optimize for bandwidth if you trust the network links between
your application and the OpenAI API.

types.description: Options for streaming response. Only set this when you set `stream: true`.

types.name: ChatCompletionTool

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

types.description: A function tool that can be used to generate a response.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletions

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the custom tool. Always `custom`.

fields.name: custom
fields.description: Properties of the custom tool.

types.description: A custom tool that processes input using a specified format.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustom

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom tool, used to identify it in tool calls.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description of the custom tool, used to provide more context.

fields.name: format
fields.description: The input format for the custom tool. Default is unconstrained text.

types.description: Properties of the custom tool.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustomFormat
types.description: The input format for the custom tool. Default is unconstrained text.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustomFormatText

fields.name: type
fields.description: Unconstrained text format. Always `text`.

types.description: Unconstrained free-form text.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustomFormatGrammar

fields.name: type
fields.description: Grammar format. Always `grammar`.

fields.name: grammar
fields.description: Your chosen grammar.

types.description: A grammar defined by the user.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustomFormatGrammarGrammar

fields.name: definition
fields.description: The grammar definition.

fields.name: syntax
fields.description: The syntax of the grammar definition. One of `lark` or `regex`.

types.description: Your chosen grammar.

types.name: CustomToolChatCompletionsCustomFormatGrammarGrammarSyntax
types.description: The syntax of the grammar definition. One of `lark` or `regex`.

types.name: ChatCompletionToolChoiceOption
types.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.
`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools.
`required` means the model must call one or more tools.
Specifying a particular tool via `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}` forces the model to call that tool.

`none` is the default when no tools are present. `auto` is the default if tools are present.

types.name: ChatCompletionToolChoiceOptionToolChoiceMode
types.description: `none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. `required` means the model must call one or more tools.

types.name: ChatCompletionAllowedToolsChoice

fields.name: type
fields.description: Allowed tool configuration type. Always `allowed_tools`.

types.description: Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

types.name: ChatCompletionAllowedTools

fields.name: mode
fields.description:
Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

`auto` allows the model to pick from among the allowed tools and generate a
message.

`required` requires the model to call one or more of the allowed tools.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
A list of tool definitions that the model should be allowed to call.

For the Chat Completions API, the list of tool definitions might look like:
```json
[
  { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "get_weather" } },
  { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "get_time" } }
]
```

types.description: Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

types.name: ChatCompletionAllowedToolsMode
types.description:
Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

`auto` allows the model to pick from among the allowed tools and generate a
message.

`required` requires the model to call one or more of the allowed tools.

types.name: ChatCompletionNamedToolChoice

fields.name: type
fields.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

types.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.name: ChatCompletionNamedToolChoiceCustom

fields.name: type
fields.description: For custom tool calling, the type is always `custom`.

types.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific custom tool.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestFunctionCall
types.description:
Deprecated in favor of `tool_choice`.

Controls which (if any) function is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a
message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a
function.

Specifying a particular function via `{"name": "my_function"}` forces the
model to call that function.

`none` is the default when no functions are present. `auto` is the default
if functions are present.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionRequestFunctionCallString
types.description: `none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a function.

types.name: ChatCompletionFunctionCallOption

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to call.

types.description: Specifying a particular function via `{"name": "my_function"}` forces the model to call that function.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionResponse

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique identifier for the chat completion.

fields.name: choices
fields.description: A list of chat completion choices. Can be more than one if `n` is greater than 1.

fields.name: created
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model used for the chat completion.

fields.name: system_fingerprint
fields.description:
This fingerprint represents the backend configuration that the model runs with.

Can be used in conjunction with the `seed` request parameter to understand when backend changes have been made that might impact determinism.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always `chat.completion`.

types.description: Represents a chat completion response returned by model, based on the provided input.

fields.name: finish_reason
fields.description:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be `stop` if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence,
`length` if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached,
`content_filter` if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters,
`tool_calls` if the model called a tool, or `function_call` (deprecated) if the model called a function.

fields.name: index
fields.description: The index of the choice in the list of choices.

types.name: CreateChatCompletionResponseChoicesItemFinishReason
types.description:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be `stop` if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence,
`length` if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached,
`content_filter` if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters,
`tool_calls` if the model called a tool, or `function_call` (deprecated) if the model called a function.

types.name: ChatCompletionResponseMessage

fields.name: annotations
fields.description:
Annotations for the message, when applicable, as when using the
[web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search?api-mode=chat).

fields.name: role
fields.description: The role of the author of this message.

fields.name: function_call
fields.description: Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`. The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

types.description: A chat completion message generated by the model.

types.name: ChatCompletionResponseMessageAnnotationsItem

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the URL citation. Always `url_citation`.

fields.name: url_citation
fields.description: A URL citation when using web search.

types.description: A URL citation when using web search.

types.name: ChatCompletionResponseMessageAnnotationsItemUrlCitation

fields.name: end_index
fields.description: The index of the last character of the URL citation in the message.

fields.name: start_index
fields.description: The index of the first character of the URL citation in the message.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL of the web resource.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the web resource.

types.description: A URL citation when using web search.

types.name: ChatCompletionResponseMessageAudio

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this audio response.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description:
The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when this audio response will
no longer be accessible on the server for use in multi-turn
conversations.

fields.name: data
fields.description:
Base64 encoded audio bytes generated by the model, in the format
specified in the request.

fields.name: transcript
fields.description: Transcript of the audio generated by the model.

types.description:
If the audio output modality is requested, this object contains data
about the audio response from the model. [Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

types.name: CreateChatCompletionResponseChoicesItemLogprobs
types.description: Log probability information for the choice.

fields.name: token
fields.description: The token.

fields.name: logprob
fields.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

fields.name: top_logprobs
fields.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position. The number of entries may be fewer than the requested `top_logprobs`.

fields.name: token
fields.description: The token.

fields.name: logprob
fields.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

types.name: CompletionUsage

fields.name: completion_tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens in the generated completion.

fields.name: prompt_tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Total number of tokens used in the request (prompt + completion).

fields.name: completion_tokens_details
fields.description: Breakdown of tokens used in a completion.

fields.name: prompt_tokens_details
fields.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt.

types.description: Usage statistics for the completion request.

types.name: CompletionUsageCompletionTokensDetails

fields.name: accepted_prediction_tokens
fields.description:
When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the
prediction that appeared in the completion.

fields.name: audio_tokens
fields.description: Audio input tokens generated by the model.

fields.name: reasoning_tokens
fields.description: Tokens generated by the model for reasoning.

fields.name: rejected_prediction_tokens
fields.description:
When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the
prediction that did not appear in the completion. However, like
reasoning tokens, these tokens are still counted in the total
completion tokens for purposes of billing, output, and context window
limits.

types.description: Breakdown of tokens used in a completion.

types.name: CompletionUsagePromptTokensDetails

fields.name: audio_tokens
fields.description: Audio input tokens present in the prompt.

fields.name: cached_tokens
fields.description: Cached tokens present in the prompt.

types.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt.

methods.description:
**Starting a new project?** We recommend trying [Responses](/docs/api-reference/responses)
to take advantage of the latest OpenAI platform features. Compare
[Chat Completions with Responses](/docs/guides/responses-vs-chat-completions?api-mode=responses).

---

Creates a model response for the given chat conversation. Learn more in the
[text generation](/docs/guides/text-generation), [vision](/docs/guides/vision),
and [audio](/docs/guides/audio) guides.

Parameter support can differ depending on the model used to generate the
response, particularly for newer reasoning models. Parameters that are only
supported for reasoning models are noted below. For the current state of
unsupported parameters in reasoning models,
[refer to the reasoning guide](/docs/guides/reasoning).

Returns a chat completion object, or a streamed sequence of chat completion
chunk objects if the request is streamed.
